feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to Configure OAuth 2.0 Authentication Between SAP Integration Suite (CPI) & S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I am working on an integration scenario where SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration/CPI) needs to consume APIs from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

Scenario

My requirement is to retrieve Sales Order data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition into SAP CPI through an iFlow. Instead of using Basic Authentication, I would like to use OAuth 2.0 for secure communication between CPI and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What configurations are required in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Integration Suite?

I am looking for guidance on the complete setup required on both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Integration Suite to enable CPI to securely consume Sales Order data using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

Any documentation references, implementation experiences, or configuration examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Value Mapping Migration from SAP PI to SAP CPI
entry.content:
We observed an issue during the migration of Value Mapping from SAP PI to SAP CPI. The source and target fields appear to have been interchanged after migration, resulting in incorrect value mapping behavior.

Could you please help us investigate and resolve this issue? We would appreciate your guidance on identifying the root cause and correcting the source-to-target mapping configuration.

entry.title: Integration Suite Service Plan Issue
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

We are encountering an issue in our integration suite.

We are trying to export a package from our Production environment and importing it in our test environment to perform some tests.

However, we are getting the following error message:

Error: Import failed. You are attempting to import an artifact type that is not supported in your current service plan. 

 

As per our understanding, we are currently on SAP Standard Edition.

Integration Suiteintegrationsuitesuccessfactors_edition2 units(none)

entry.title: Roles For Adding Capabilities in CPI System
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

Please suggest relevant roles for adding the below capabilities in CPI system.

  • API Management
  • Open Connectors
  • Integration Advisor
  • Integration Assessment

Also attaching same document for reference

entry.title: Integration Suite - UNA segment is not generating for the receiver payload
entry.content:
We are currently working with SAP IDoc to EDIFACT-related maps in mapping services. We used the Standard Message Definition (D.01B S3 ORDRSP/DESADV/INVOIC) for the mapping. However, during testing, we noticed that there was no UNA in the output.
We would like to inquire:

  1. Is UNA auto-generated by default? If so, why is it not being generated with our mapping services?
  2. If UNA is not auto-generated, is there any way to enable auto-generation?

 We simply want to include (UNA:+.?*') at the beginning of the output.

entry.title: issue to deploy 3 copies of the standard iflows package in single CPI tenant for the 3 systems
entry.content:
Dear SAP support,

We are facing an issue to deploy 3 copies of the standard iflows package in single CPI tenant for the 3 systems Dev and QAS and Live-Production.
 
The targeted standard iflows :
Replicate Organizational Objects from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA
Replicate Employee Master Data and Org. Assignments from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA
 
The Steps that Performed:
3 Copies created from the package “SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Integration with SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA: Employee and Organizational Data”
The Configure data has completed for the all iflows
The SF credintials and url replaced with  ${property.CREDENTIALS_SFSF} and ${property.ADDRESS_SFSF} because connectd to multiple Employee Central instances
A copy created from value mapping ”Employee Central Instance ID Mapping”  
The issue :
Not able to use value mapping ”Employee Central Instance ID Mapping” for the 3 system, due to that they have the same Source value (EC Instance Id) but different Target value (URL & SFSF Credentials )

entry.title: Issue with the SOAP Envelop tags
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

There is an interface SOAP synch from source. while sending the response back , BTP automatically adding the SOAP env as below
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
But source system is expecting the tag as 

<soap-env:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">

Can you please let me know what is the solution for this. Please note the source adapter has to be SOAP synch

entry.title: HTTP 403 Forbidden when SAP CPI calls On-Premise HTTPS API via Cloud Connector.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to connect SAP Integration Suite (CPI) to a third-party REST API that is hosted on an on-premise server.

The third party has exposed the API over HTTPS with port 443, and the API works successfully when called directly from Postman using the HTTPS URL. This API does not require any kind of headers. 

To access the on-premise system from CPI, I am using SAP Cloud Connector. In my integration flow, I have configured the receiver using the Cloud Connector virtual host. 
I am using HTTP receiver adapter with HTTP protocol because Proxy type On-Premise does not allow HTTPS.
We are calling API with HTTP  protocol and port 80.
Has anyone faced a similar issue where an HTTPS API works from Postman but returns 403 only when called through SAP Cloud Connector with HTTP protocol?


Cloud connector is Reachable.

entry.title: Partner Function Replication Issue in C4C V2: Sales Area–Dependent Account Team Members Overwritten
entry.content:
We are currently replicating Business Partner data from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) V2 via CPI. The requirement is to maintain partner functions exclusively in C4C, so we have disabled the Partner Function node in the CPI message mapping.

However, after this change, we observed unexpected behavior during replication:
with every Business Partner update from S/4HANA, only the Account Team Members that have a Sales Area dependency are being removed/overwritten, while other Account Team Members (without Sales Area dependency) remain unchanged.

Test Scenario:

  • A Business Partner exists in C4C with three Account Team Members:
    • Some with Sales Area dependency
    • Some without Sales Area dependency

  • A change is made in S/4HANA for this Business Partner
  • The replication to C4C is triggered again

Observed Result:

  • Account Team Members with Sales Area dependency are deleted/overwritten
  • Account Team Members without Sales Area dependency remain intact
  • This occurs even though the Partner Function node is not included in the payload

 

Has anyone encountered a similar behavior when replicating Business Partner data to C4C V2?
What would be the recommended approach to prevent the Sales Area–dependent Account Team Members from being overwritten during replication?

entry.title: Not able to find the default Event Mesh in Entitlements in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We are planning to use default Event Mesh along with the Integration Suite. However, we tried to entitle that Either form Global Account or Subaccount we are not able to find the service.

Could you please help.

entry.title: SAP CPI OData V4 POST – Invalid CSRF Token Error for WarehouseTask Confirm API (EWM A2X)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working with the standard SAP EWM API – Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), specifically using the WarehouseTask entity. This is an OData V4 API.

🔹 Scenario

I am trying to perform a POST operation to confirm a Warehouse Task using the below endpoint:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask/EU20/1234563436/0/SAP__self.ConfirmWarehouseTaskHndlgUnit

Where:

  • EU20 → EWM Warehouse
  • 1234563436 → Warehouse Task
  • 0 → Warehouse Task Item

🔹 Approach Followed

  1. First, I executed a GET call on the same API to:

    • Fetch the CSRF token
    • Retrieve the corresponding ETag for the specific combination (Warehouse, WarehouseTask, WarehouseTaskItem)in the body.

  2. For the POST request:

    • I used the same CSRF token in the header
    • Set the header:
      If-Match = <ETag (from GET response)>

🔹 Issue Faced

While triggering the POST request, I am receiving the following error:

Invalid CSRF token

🔹 Observed Behavior

  • In the CPI trace logs (receiver side), I can see that the CSRF token is being passed in the request header.
  • However, after the failure:
    • In the request headers attachment, the CSRF token appears as "fetch" instead of the actual token value.

This is creating a confusing situation, as the token seems to be passed but still results in an "Invalid CSRF token" error.

🔹 API Reference

For reference, I am using the API from SAP Business Accelerator Hub:
Warehouse Task Confirm API

🔹 Ask

Has anyone encountered a similar issue while working with this API or OData V4 CSRF handling in CPI?

Any guidance on:

  • Correct CSRF handling approach, or
  • Possible reasons why the token is getting replaced with "fetch"
    would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: CPI With Azure Bucket
entry.content:
We have noticed that Azure Storage occasionally changes the bucket ID. When this happens, the integration stops working until I perform a connectivity test without credentials to retrieve the new bucket ID. After that, I have to update the known.hosts file with the new ID before the connection works again.

Is there a recommended approach to avoid this issue? Specifically, is it possible to configure the connection without relying on the bucket ID, or is there a more stable authentication or configuration method that prevents this from happening?

entry.title: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online – Certificate-Based OAuth2 Client Credentials
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently implementing an integration between SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) and Microsoft SharePoint Online.

The SharePoint team has confirmed the following requirements:

  • Direct Client Certificate Authentication (mTLS) is not supported.
  • Authentication must be performed via Microsoft Entra ID App Registration.
  • Client Secret authentication is not preferred due to password expiry and security requirements.
  • Certificate-Based OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication is the recommended approach.

While evaluating the standard authentication options available in SAP CPI, we could not identify an out-of-the-box configuration for OAuth2 Client Credentials using certificate-based authentication (Private Key JWT / JWT Assertion) by leveraging an existing CPI Key Pair (certificate and private key).

The options currently available in our CPI tenant are:

  • OAuth2 Client Credentials
  • OAuth2 Authorization Code
  • OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion
  • Client Certificate Authentication

However, we could not find a standard way to configure OAuth2 Client Credentials with certificate/private key mapping for Microsoft Entra ID without implementing custom JWT generation logic through Groovy scripting.

I would appreciate feedback from anyone who has worked on a similar scenario.

Questions:

  1. Has anyone successfully implemented SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online integration using Microsoft Entra ID App Registration and certificate-based OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication?

  2. Were you able to achieve this using only standard CPI capabilities?

  3. If custom development was required, did you implement JWT generation through Groovy scripting or use an alternative approach?

  4. Has SAP provided any standard capability, roadmap item, SAP Note, or recommended approach for this authentication pattern?

  5. What is the generally accepted best practice for SAP CPI integrations where Microsoft Entra ID requires certificate-based authentication instead of client secret authentication?

Any guidance, implementation experience, SAP Notes, blogs, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: upgrade Flexso TDD integration suite environment to enhanced edition
entry.content:
We would like to upgrade our TDD Integration Suite environment to "enhanced edition", in order to get hands-on access to new MCP gateway component. 
What is the procedure to get this going?

entry.title: Change color of DEV, QAS & PRD BTP's Integration Suite portals
entry.content:
Hello SAP BTP support,

Is there a way to change colors of our BTP IS environments in a web-browser? It is very confusing to have all of them identical as many people make mistakes when working with 2 or all 3 environments. 

Please let me know how can I change color?

Many thanks,,

MAC

entry.title: Looking for ODATA API from SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
Dear SAP, 

We are looking to GET these fields as part of ODATA API from SuccessFactors LMS System.

Here are the fields.

SFUserAssessmentData
    
                        name=UserID
                             name=ItemID
                            name=Iteration
                            name=LastAccessDate
                             name=Finished
                           name=Complete
                            name=CompletionDate
                            name=QuestionsAsked
                             name=QuestionsCorrect
                            name=Evaluation
                          name=ExamEvaluation

 

Let us know the ODATA API to be used in pulling these data from SF LMS.

entry.title: Inquiry about boolean value support in SAP CPI for Regex Filtering setting
entry.content: I am trying to understand why setting the Regex Filtering to true in SAP CPI does not work, but setting it to 1 works. I want to know if SAP CPI supports boolean values as true or false or if it accepts 1/0. I observed that when I set to true, it didn't pick the file, but when I set it to 1, it started working. I need clarification on supported boolean value formats in SAP CPI for this setting.

entry.title: Maximum Pool Size 10までの制限について
entry.content:
以下のサイトに記載されておりますコネクションプールの設定について確認させて下さい。

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/managing-jdbc-data-sources?utm_source=chatgpt.com&locale=en-US

上記サイトには以下のような記載があるのですが、

Maximum Pool Size

The upper limit on the number of database connections maintained by the connection pool, including active and idle connections.

It controls peak resource usage and prevents database server overload. Default value: 10 Valid range: 1 to database-supported connection limit

POCのExit Criteriaを満たす上でMaximum Po

entry.title: Is this a known limitation or defect in Trial tenants?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am working with SAP Integration Suite Trial (Cloud Integration v8.44.19) and trying to use the native Git integration for my Integration Flow artifacts.

I have been investigating this issue for quite some time and have performed extensive root cause analysis before posting here.

Environment

  • SAP Integration Suite Trial
  • Cloud Integration Version: 8.44.19
  • GitHub Repository: Public
  • GitHub Personal Access Token: Fine-grained PAT
  • CI/CD Service: Subscribed and role collections assigned
  • Package: Created from scratch (not imported from ZIP)
  • Integration Flow: Created from scratch

    Problem

    Whenever I perform Git Push, I immediately receive the following backend response:

     

     
    {
      "errorCode": "REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND",
      "errorMessage": "The artifact child doesn't contain the repository information"
    }
     Sometimes I also receive:

     

     
    This artifact is not associated with a Git repository.
    Git operations can't be performed.
     

    What I have already verified

    GitHub

    • Repository exists.
    • Repository is accessible.
    • Repository is public.
    • PAT is valid.
    • PAT has the required permissions.

      SAP Git Access

      Git Access configuration is successful.

      When opening Git Push:

      • Repository dropdown is populated correctly.
      • Selecting the repository automatically loads the branches.
      • Branch selection works correctly.

        Therefore, GitHub connectivity appears to be working.

        Browser Developer Tools

        Using Chrome Developer Tools (Network tab), I observed:

        • Repository list is fetched successfully.
        • Branch list is fetched successfully.
        • Push request immediately fails with

           

           
          REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND
           UI Investigation

          While debugging the SAP UI JavaScript, I noticed the Git Push controller expects repository metadata through:

           

           
          this.repoDetails.repoName
          this.repoDetails.repoURL
          this.repoDetails.branch
           The controller also validates:

           

           
          if (!oModelData.repoName ||
              !oModelData.repoURL ||
              !oModelData.branch)
          {
              sap.m.MessageToast.show("repository configuration not found");
          }
           This suggests the frontend expects repository metadata to already exist for the artifact.

          Previous errors observed

          During earlier troubleshooting I also encountered:

           

           
          GIT_OPERATION_FORBIDDEN
           
           
          repository configuration not found
           
           
          This artifact is not associated with a Git repository.
           Eventually the backend consistently reports:

           

           
          REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND
           Root Cause Analysis Performed

          Based on my investigation, I believe the following have already been ruled out:

          • GitHub connectivity
          • Personal Access Token
          • Repository visibility
          • Branch availability
          • Repository configuration
          • Package creation from ZIP
          • Imported artifacts

            The repository and branch can both be fetched successfully, which suggests GitHub communication is functioning correctly.

            The failure appears to occur because the backend cannot find Git repository metadata associated with the Integration Flow artifact.

            My Question

            Has anyone experienced this issue on SAP Integration Suite Trial?

            Specifically:

            1. Is this a known limitation or defect in Trial tenants?
            2. How is the repository metadata associated with an Integration Flow/package?
            3. Is there any supported way to recreate or repair the missing repository association?
            4. Has anyone successfully resolved REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND for Git Push in Cloud Integration Trial?

              Any guidance or references to SAP Notes or official documentation would be greatly appreciated.

              Thank you.

entry.title: how to read SAP Application Message Type in integration
entry.content:
Hello all,

I'm working in an integration where I need to read the property "SAP Application Message Type" to apply its value in a router.

I've set the condition as "${header.SAP_MessageType}" but it's not working. Could you help to clarify this, please?

Thanks!
